scope_note: Princeton's official testing page explains both the current temporary test-optional policy and the planned return to required SAT/ACT testing for the next cycle. Princeton does not publish per-major testing ranges here, so this panel stays school-wide.
policy: Princeton is test-optional for first-year applicants seeking fall 2026 or fall 2027 entry, and it will return to requiring the SAT or ACT for applicants seeking fall 2028 entry.

claims.claim: Princeton will return to requiring standardized testing for undergraduate admission beginning with the 2027-28 admission cycle.
First-year applicants seeking to enroll in fall 2028 will need to submit either SAT or ACT scores.
There are no minimum test score requirements for admission.
Princeton has no preference for the SAT versus the ACT.
